title: Here are four under the radar Red Sox players standing out in spring training
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Braiden Ward impresses in Red Sox spring training with 10 hits and 12 stolen bases in 12 games.
Max Ferguson leads the Red Sox in RBIs with seven, showing versatility across positions in spring training.
Mickey Gasper returns to the Red Sox, adding depth at catcher with six hits and five RBIs this spring.

title: Once viewed as a rental, could Viktor Arvidsson be ideal ‘bridge’ forward for Bruins?
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Viktor Arvidsson, 32, is second on the Boston Bruins in 5-on-5 goals, solidifying his role on the team.
Arvidsson cleared the trade deadline without being moved, despite his pending free agent status and interest from other teams.
The Bruins are considering a potential three-year extension for Arvidsson as a bridge forward for their developing lineup.

title: 5 interior offensive linemen options for Patriots after Garrett Bradbury trade
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Patriots plan to shift Jared Wilson from left guard to center after trading Garrett Bradbury to the Bears.
Potential options for Patriots at left guard include free agents Isaac Seumalo and David Edwards.
Elgton Jenkins and Joel Bitonio are possible linemen options, plus draft prospect Emmanuel Pregnon from Oregon.

title: He’s running the Boston Marathon for the ‘amazing’ Make-A-Wish children
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Jason Perry from Stockton, California is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support Make-A-Wish children.
Perry emphasizes the inspiration he draws from the resilience and hope of Wish children despite their challenges.
Over 15 years, Perry has experienced meaningful interactions with Wish children, who motivate him to raise funds and awareness.

title: Insider predicts Patriots sign Seahawks standout pass rusher, but don’t land Alec Pierce in free agency
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Patriots predicted to sign Seahawks edge rusher Boye Mafe for three years, $48 million, $25 million guaranteed.
Patriots unlikely to sign Colts wide receiver Alec Pierce, projected to join Chargers with a three-year, $80 million deal.
New England has $61.6 million in cap space, aiming to strengthen their roster in free agency.

title: Review & setlist: With sharp sarcasm, Jesse Welles took on war, bullies, and Trump at the House of Blues
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Jesse Welles performed at the House of Blues Boston on March 6, 2026, part of his 'Under the Powerlines Tour.'
Welles played 20 songs, including originals and covers, with a focus on political satire and social commentary.
Despite Grammy nominations, Welles is recognized for his sharp lyrical critiques and engaging live performances.

title: Masataka Yoshida hits a home run in Japan’s win vs. Korea, with Drake Maye in attendance
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Masataka Yoshida hit a home run and had three RBIs in Japan's 8-6 win over Korea in the World Baseball Classic.
Red Sox outfielder Yoshida delivered a two-run single in the seventh inning, extending Japan's lead to 8-5.
Patriots quarterback Drake Maye attended the game in Tokyo, sporting a Dodgers uniform.

title: This Roslindale native is running her first Boston Marathon for Mass. Eye and Ear
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Roslindale native Makayla Aboujaoude-Quirk is running her first Boston Marathon in 2026 at age 25.
She is participating with Team Eye and Ear to support Mass Eye and Ear, where she was a patient for nearly 10 years.
Mass Eye and Ear is noted for its research and the only 24-hour emergency service for eye, neck, ear, nose, and throat trauma in Massachusetts.

title: It was overdue, but Tim Legler was promoted to ESPN/ABC’s top NBA broadcast team this season, and he’s thriving
author: Chad Finn
bullets: Tim Legler, an ESPN/ABC analyst since 2000, now serves on their top NBA broadcast team with Mike Breen and Richard Jefferson.
Legler's promotion comes after instability in the broadcast team, with previous analysts departing for NBA coaching roles.
Legler praises Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla and highlights Jaylen Brown's rise, while anticipating Jayson Tatum's return from injury.

title: Cooper Flagg was the Maine story in Jayson Tatum’s return
author: Chad Finn
bullets: Cooper Flagg scored 16 points, 8 rebounds, and 6 assists in his TD Garden NBA debut for the Dallas Mavericks.
Celtics' Jayson Tatum returned after a 298-day Achilles recovery with 15 points, 12 rebounds, and 7 assists.
Flagg, a Maine native, received a warm reception from the Boston crowd during his first NBA game at TD Garden.
